message:
fix(mux-client): replace cancel() with timeout(shutdown()) for graceful subscription cleanup

Subscription teardown previously used `sub.cancel()` which drops the
subscription handle immediately without waiting for the background task
to acknowledge the cancellation. This could leave the mock server's
accept loop in a race where it tries to send into a half-closed channel.

All test sites now use `timeout(Duration::from_millis(500), sub.shutdown())`
which:
1. Signals the subscription to stop accepting new events
2. Waits for the background reader to drain and exit cleanly
3. Fails the test explicitly if shutdown hangs (instead of silently leaking)

Assertions that depended on ordering relative to cancel are moved before
the shutdown call where they were already logically evaluated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
